title: Not Good Enough
lyrics: I'm gonna run away, if you like you, I'm gonna lose all my patience and not stay, not today, but you gotta see yourself in a view that you can understand, and no one else sees that, or plans that, or knows that, except you, and when you don't say any of that, how do you know, how do you know what others feel, or are you aware of anything other than narcissistic flair, because if you're found, then you're found, and you won't second guess, feel different ways, feel guilt, tell everybody else they're this way, and that they're not good enough to pass your own test, well neither are you, and that's the way that you critically be in tune with you, because no one's good enough for you, but neither are you, so just relate, instead of taking it down to the bottom of all your fears and dreams, that you can't put to the forefront, the things that you think define your life, what defines you, no to you, cause you say you know, and you say you know it all, better than the last one, can't you see that, I'm just a fucking mess, and I've never organized anything before, oh, no you just couldn't find it there on the floor, because I put different weights in me, different things that make me tick, and I don't mean to butthurt, even though you're a butthead, yeah, you don't think I'm good enough, gotta, gotta criticize instead of teach, it's only what you've been taught, yeah, yeah, no because I'm not good enough, but neither are you, so why are you making it your point of view, or something to stand attuned to baby, cause you've only got this one chance to view you, in the light of nothing other than the dark, that your heart tells you you've been, and all life is tourist, why don't you build flowers over there, take your cactus, make a meadow that's better than you ever made it before, don't just hate and criticize others, it just shows you're weak, and that you don't think you're good enough, because when you're good enough, you don't criticize, you teach.
